title: 10 best herbal teas for oily hair
intro: Several herbal teas, known for their oil-absorbing and clarifying properties, stand out as excellent choices for addressing oily hair. These include chamomile, calendula, and nettle tea. Chamomile tea helps normalize sebum production while providing soothing anti-inflammatory benefits. Calendula tea, rich in flavonoids, gently cleanses and soothes the scalp. Nettle tea, a potent astringent, reduces oiliness and promotes a balanced scalp environment. Utilizing these natural herbal remedies not only addresses oily hair concerns but also fosters hair and scalp health.

teas.tea_desc: Green tea's tannins and catechins naturally combat oily hair by gently stripping excess sebum from the scalp and hair follicles. This herbal remedy also contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that soothe irritation and regulate scalp oil production. Furthermore, green tea's high antioxidant content promotes hair growth and provides a balanced environment for healthier, shinier hair. By rinsing hair with cooled green tea, the caffeine in it stimulates hair follicles, resulting in stronger strands and reduced oiliness. Ultimately, green tea's natural compounds work synergistically to address the root cause of oily hair, making it an effective and sustainable solution for maintaining optimal scalp and hair health.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of catechins and polyphenols that help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Buds: Rich in epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG) and other catechins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and balance oily scalps.
Stems: Packed with antioxidants and flavonoids that naturally cleanse and remove excess oil from the hair and scalp.
Seeds: Contain linoleic and oleic acids, which nourish the hair and help control sebum production when used as a medicinal tea.
Flowers: High in vitamin C and E, providing nourishment and strengthening the hair follicles, while helping to balance sebum production.
tea_constituents: Caffeine: Stimulates hair follicles, reducing excess oil production.
EGCG: Reduces sebum secretion, preventing greasy hair.
Theobromine: Promotes hair growth, reducing need for frequent washing.
Theaflavins: Possesses antimicrobial properties, eliminating bacteria causing oily hair.
Thearubigins: Acts as an antioxidant, protecting hair from damage caused by excess sebum.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 2 bags of green tea for 15 minutes.
In a separate container, mix 1 cup of cooled green tea with 2 drops of tea tree oil.
Shampoo oily hair as normal, then rinse thoroughly.
Pour the cooled green tea and tea tree oil mixture over hair, massaging into scalp.
Leave on for 10 minutes, then rinse out with cool water.

teas.tea_desc: Rosemary tea helps with oily hair because it contains carnosic acid, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces excess sebum production in the scalp. Furthermore, rosemary tea stimulates circulation, ensuring that nutrients reach hair follicles efficiently. Its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties also promote a healthy scalp environment, preventing dandruff and other related issues. Regular use of rosemary tea as a hair rinse can lead to balanced oil production, shinier, and more voluminous hair without resorting to harsh chemical treatments. The astringent qualities of rosemary tea tighten pores in the scalp, providing long-term benefits for managing oily hair.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of anti-inflammatory compounds that help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants that promote hair growth and soothe an oily scalp.
Stems: Contain essential oils that naturally balance oil production and stimulate hair growth.
Seeds: Packed with nutrients that nourish the hair and help control excess oil production.
tea_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production.
Carnosic acid: Exhibits antioxidant effects, aiding in the balance of oiliness in the scalp.
Ursolic acid: Known for its antimicrobial properties, helping control scalp microflora that can lead to oily hair.
Oleanolic acid: Demonstrates anti-androgenic effects, which can help reduce hair follicle oil production.
Verbascoside: Shows sebum-regulating properties, assisting in managing oily hair.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water, then add 2 sprigs of fresh rosemary and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove rosemary from the pot and let the tea cool down to a warm temperature.
Mix 2 tablespoons of apple cider vinegar into the rosemary tea.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then pour the rosemary tea mixture over your hair, massaging it into your scalp.
Leave the tea in your hair for 10 minutes before rinsing it out thoroughly.

teas.tea_desc: Sage tea offers a natural solution for oily hair due to its astringent properties, which tighten pores and reduce excess oil production. This herbal tea's antibacterial qualities combat the growth of microorganisms that cause scalp irritation and further oil secretion. Moreover, sage tea contains essential nutrients and minerals that nourish hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and maintaining an adequate oil balance. Regular use of sage tea as a rinse or hair treatment effectively eliminates impurities, leaving the hair feeling clean and refreshed. Thus, the shrub's potent qualities combat greasiness, unveiling a revitalized, radiant appearance.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have astringent properties that help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Stems: Contain beneficial compounds that can help balance oil production and promote hair growth.
Buds: Rich in plant nutrients, including antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, that can help regulate oil production in the scalp.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the hair and help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Roots: Contain active compounds that can help balance oil production in the scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
tea_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in hair.
Carnosic acid: A powerful antioxidant that promotes hair growth and scalp health, while controlling excess oil secretion.
Ursolic acid: A compound with anti-androgen properties that inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, reducing hair follicle oil production and dHT levels.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, helping to balance scalp oil levels and maintain a healthy scalp.
Camphor: A natural compound with cooling and stimulating properties that improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness.
Tannins: Astringent compounds that help to remove excess oil from the scalp and hair, while soothing irritation and reducing inflammation.
tea_recipe: Gather 4-5 fresh sage leaves, 2 cups of water, and a strainer.
Bring water to a boil, then add sage leaves and lower heat to a simmer.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract sage's properties.
Remove from heat and strain the liquid into a separate container.
Allow the sage tea to cool down before massaging it into your oily hair and scalp.

teas.tea_desc: Peppermint tea contains anti-sebum and anti-microbial properties, which reduce excess oil production and tackle scalp bacteria. Its soothing qualities alleviate inflammation and irritation, promoting a healthy scalp environment. Furthermore, peppermint tea stimulates circulation, encouraging hair growth and rejuvenation. By rinsing hair with peppermint tea, you eliminate residue from hair products, leaving hair clean and refreshed. Ultimately, the astringent nature of peppermint tea helps balance oil production, resulting in healthier, more manageable hair.
tea_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and flavonoids that help regulate sebum production in the scalp, preventing oily hair.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils that stimulate hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and fatty acids that nourish the scalp, preventing excess oil production and maintaining healthy hair.
tea_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a natural cleanser that helps balance the scalp's oil production, preventing excess grease buildup.
Mentone: Contributes to reducing inflammation and itching, common issues in oily hair due to overactive sebaceous glands.
Limonene: Known for its antifungal and antibacterial properties, it helps maintain a healthy and non-greasy scalp.
Methyl acetate: Improves blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oil buildup.
β-Caryophyllene: Provides soothing and anti-inflammatory effects on the scalp, alleviating irritation caused by sebum overproduction.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the peppermint leaves, pouring it into a clean spray bottle.
Shampoo and rinse your hair as usual, then spray the peppermint tea onto your scalp and massage it in.
Leave the tea on your hair for 10-15 minutes before rinsing it out with cold water.

teas.tea_desc: Nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that aid in regulating the scalp's oil production. These properties tighten the scalp's pores, reducing excess sebum secretion. Furthermore, nettle tea includes nutrients such as vitamin C, which promotes hair growth and enhances hair quality, while balancing the scalp's natural oil levels. Additionally, it nourishes the hair follicles, preventing excessive oil buildup and leaving the hair feeling fresh and clean. Drinking nettle tea also supports the body's internal balance, contributing to healthier skin and hair.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and known for their astringent properties, leaves help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Stems: Packed with silica and antioxidants, stems promote hair strength and shine while balancing oiliness.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, flowers soothe the scalp, reducing excess oil and dandruff.
Seeds: Seeds are a powerhouse of fatty acids and vitamin E, deeply moisturizing hair without leaving any greasy residue.
Roots: High in antioxidants and minerals, roots help reduce scalp irritation, balance pH levels, and control oil production.
tea_constituents: Selenium: Essential for maintaining healthy scalp by regulating sebum production.
Silica: Promotes hair strength and shine, helps prevent oily build-up.
Iodine: Supports thyroid function, reducing excess oil production.
Vitamin C: Boosts collagen production, improves hair structure and reduces greasiness.
Chlorophyll: Helps balance hormones and reduces oil production.
Beta-carotene: Converts into Vitamin A, promoting healthy sebum production and hair growth.
tea_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh nettle leaves, rinse them, and place in a pot.
Add 3 cups of water to the pot, bring to a boil, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a bowl, discarding the leaves, and let the tea cool.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the cooled nettle tea with 1 cup of water in a spray bottle.
Apply the mixture evenly to oily hair, let it sit for 15 minutes, then rinse thoroughly.

teas.tea_desc: Burdock tea promotes hair health by nourishing the scalp with essential nutrients and minerals, as it contains high levels of phytosterols and fatty acids. Its anti-inflammatory and anti-bacterial properties help in soothing the scalp, reducing excessive oil production, and alleviating dandruff. Additionally, burdock tea stimulates blood circulation, facilitating the efficient distribution of nutrients and elimination of waste products, allowing for healthier hair growth. The tea's natural astringent properties help tighten the scalp's pores, reducing sebum production, contributing to less oily hair. Consequently, burdock tea's comprehensive benefits for hair and scalp health lead to improved hair quality and reduced oiliness.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in fatty acids, the roots help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Rhyzomes: Contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that promote hair growth and soothe irritated scalp.
Leaves: High in tannins, the leaves help balance scalp oils and reduce hair greasiness.
Seeds: Full of essential fatty acids, the seeds nourish the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness.
Buds: Abundant in phytosterols, the buds strengthen hair strands, preventing excess oil build-up on the scalp.
tea_constituents: Tannins: Tannins are astringent compounds that help to remove excess oil from the scalp and hair, reducing the appearance of oily hair.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols in burdock tea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe an irritated scalp, reducing oil production and improving the health of the hair.
Sterols: Sterols found in burdock tea can help to regulate sebum production, reducing excessive oiliness in the hair.
Volatile oils: Volatile oils in burdock tea, such as linalool and camphor, have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help to maintain a healthy scalp and reduce oiliness.
Mucilage: Mucilage in burdock tea has emollient properties that can help to soften and condition the hair, reducing the need for excessive styling products that can contribute to oily hair.
tea_recipe: Chop 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root into small pieces, place in a pot, and add 2 cups of water.
Bring mixture to a boil, then lower heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a bowl, discarding the burdock pieces.
Allow tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Pour tea slowly over your hair, massaging it into the scalp and leaving it on for 15 minutes before rinsing.

teas.tea_desc: Horsetail tea offers a natural solution for oily hair because it contains silica, a mineral that promotes hair strength and health. This silica content helps to regulate the production of sebum, the oily substance responsible for oily hair. As a result, horsetail tea reduces excess sebum, leading to balanced hair with less oiliness. Moreover, horsetail's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties support a healthy scalp, further contributing to the reduction of oiliness in hair. Ultimately, drinking horsetail tea addresses the root cause of oily hair, while rinses or direct applications can provide additional benefits.
tea_parts: Rhyzomes: Contain silica, a mineral that can help to balance oil production in the scalp.
Leaves: Rich in saponins, natural detergents that can help to cleanse and remove excess oil from the hair.
Stems: Have astringent properties that can help to tone and reduce excess oil on the scalp.
Seeds: Contain flavonoids and essential fatty acids that can help to nourish and strengthen the hair, preventing excess oil production.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing excess oil production.
tea_constituents: Silica: Strengthens hair follicles, helps to absorb excess oil and stimulates hair growth.
Selenium: Promotes hair growth and reduces dandruff caused by oily hair.
Potassium: Helps to regulate the production of sebum, reducing oiliness in the scalp.
Manganese: Promotes healthy hair growth by improving nutrient absorption in the scalp.
Caffeic acid: Reduces inflammation and itchiness in the scalp, improving overall hair health.
Tannins: Act as a natural astringent, helping to reduce excess oil production in the scalp.
tea_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb, letting it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Filter - Strain the tea to remove horsetail herb particles, reserving the liquid.
Cool - Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Mix - Combine 1/4 cup of horsetail tea with 3/4 cup of water in a spray bottle.
Apply - Spray the horsetail tea mixture liberally onto oily hair, massaging it gently into the scalp, and letting it air dry.

teas.tea_desc: Lavender tea offers a natural solution for oily hair due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help regulate sebum production and soothe scalp irritations. This herbal infusion contains essential oils and antioxidants known to balance the scalp's natural oil levels, preventing excessive secretion and promoting healthier-looking hair. The calming effect of lavender also aids in reducing stress, a common trigger for increased sebum production, indirectly contributing to the reduction of oily hair. Furthermore, lavender tea's gentle astringent properties help cleanse and tighten pores, removing impurities and build-up that can exacerbate oily hair conditions. Regular use of lavender tea as a hair rinse promotes a balanced, revitalized scalp and lustrous, manageable hair.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain essential oils with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help regulate sebum production.
Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and tannins that gently cleanse and soothe the scalp, balancing oiliness.
Buds: High in flavonoids, they promote hair growth and provide natural conditioning for oily hair.
tea_constituents: Linalyl Acetate: A compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps balance sebum production in the scalp, reducing oiliness in hair.
Linalool: A natural monoterpene alcohol that exhibits antiseptic and antifungal properties, helping to maintain a clean and healthy scalp.
Eucalyptol: A compound known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated scalps.
Camphor: A cyclic ketone with a cooling sensation and antimicrobial properties, aiding in the reduction of excess oil in hair and promoting a balanced scalp environment.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried culinary lavender, letting it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the lavender tea into a clean container, discarding the used flowers.
Allow the tea to cool down to a warm temperature.
Mix 1/4 cup of the lavender tea with 3/4 cup of cool water in a spray bottle.
Evenly distribute the lavender tea mixture onto damp hair, massaging it into the scalp and leaving it on for 15 minutes before rinsing thoroughly.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea is a natural ingredient known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It helps to soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting overall scalp health. Additionally, chamomile tea contains antioxidants that can help to regulate oil production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in the hair. By rinsing hair with chamomile tea, it can help to brighten highlights and leave hair soft and shiny. Regular use of chamomile tea as a hair rinse contributes to a balanced scalp and healthier, more manageable hair.
tea_parts: Flowers: Contain volatile oils and antioxidants that help regulate sebum production in the scalp, promoting balanced hair oils.
Leaves: Rich in nourishing compounds that gently cleanse oily hair, leaving it refreshed and revitalized.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids that strengthen hair follicles, reducing excess oiliness and promoting healthy hair growth.
tea_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that helps regulate sebum production, preventing excessive oiliness in the hair.
Chamazulene: A volatile oil with anti-inflammatory properties, soothing the scalp and reducing excess oil production.
Bisabolol: A terpene alcohol that has anti-inflammatory and anti-microbial effects, helping to balance scalp oils and promote healthy hair.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene, matricin provides anti-inflammatory benefits, reducing scalp irritation and excess oil production.
Spiroethers: Compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, they help maintain a healthy scalp and support balanced sebum production.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and steep 4 chamomile tea bags for 15 minutes to extract the oil-reducing properties.
In a separate container, mix 2 tablespoons of honey, known for its moisturizing benefits, with 2 tablespoons of cooled chamomile tea.
After shampooing, pour the chamomile-honey mixture through your hair, focusing on the oiliest areas.
Leave the mixture on for 10 minutes to allow the nutrients to penetrate the hair follicles.
Rinse your hair thoroughly with warm water, then style as usual.

teas.tea_desc: Calendula tea, made from marigold flowers, contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties help regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp. Additionally, calendula tea's antimicrobial qualities combat scalp bacteria, further benefiting oily hair. The tea's gentle astringent effect helps cleanse and tighten pores, preventing further oil buildup. Furthermore, calendula tea stimulates hair growth and strengthens hair follicles, promoting overall hair health and reducing oiliness.
tea_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, calendula flowers help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Leaves: Contain triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate irritation and dandruff caused by oily hair.
Stems: Have high levels of antioxidants that promote hair growth and maintain a healthy scalp by reducing oxidative stress.
Seeds: Contain fatty acids and phytosterols, which nourish the scalp, strengthen hair follicles, and help control oil production.
tea_constituents: Saponins: These naturally occurring compounds have a cleaning effect on the hair and scalp, helping to remove excess oil.
Flavonoids: Acting as powerful antioxidants, flavonoids help regulate sebum production, reducing greasiness in the hair.
Triterpenes: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, triterpenes soothe the scalp, alleviating irritation often associated with oily hair.
Carotenoids: These pigments, responsible for the vibrant color of calendula, have vitamin A-like activity that can help regulate sebum production and maintain a balanced scalp.
tea_recipe: Chop 2 tablespoons of dried calendula flowers and place them in a pot.
Pour 2 cups of water into the pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the solids, and let it cool.
Massage the cooled tea into your oily hair, leave it on for 30 minutes, then rinse thoroughly.
